arts.ttl: Man Utd without EIGHT stars for Ipswich Town clash as Ruben Amorim faces crisis
arts.sum: Manchester United's fitness problems are piling up after losing both Amad Diallo and Lisandro Martinez to season-ending injuries, with Ruben Amorim facing a crisis for the Ipswich Town clash

arts.ttl: Amorim feels Manchester United staff ‘paying the price’ for poor on-field form
arts.sum: Manager says recruitment and team needs to improve Return of Luke Shaw and Mason Mount unknown Ruben Amorim believes Manchester United employees made redundant are “paying the price” for poor on‑field performance and that recruitment has to improve to address the problem. Omar Berrada, United’s chief executive, confirmed on Monday that Sir Jim Ratcliffe is to cut up to another 200 jobs after 250 people were made unemployed last year. Amorim was asked how the remaining staff and fans could be confident this will help the club to prosper. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Read Ruben's quotes on Dorgu, Hojlund, Mount & Shaw
arts.sum: Managers who have been here in the past have talked about the weight of the shirt, but in this situation where people are losing their jobs, having to sit in front of us and talk about added pressure and responsibilities, is that not an environment that’s making it really, really hard for these players – particularly young players – to play and perform and to really be themselves? “I’m not saying it’s not a bad thing but I think the players don’t feel that too much.

arts.ttl: Ruben Amorim reveals Man Utd dressing room's response to brutal job cuts
arts.sum: Manchester United are set to make up to 200 more staff redundant, on top of 250 job cuts last year, as part of an ongoing effort to address £300million losses over the last three years

arts.ttl: 'They live in a bubble' - what Manchester United players make of job cuts
arts.sum: Up to 200 more staff members could be made redundant by Man United amid the team's flagging form.

arts.ttl: Man United's players AREN'T feeling the pressure of Ineos' redundancies at the club because they live in 'a bubble', claims boss Ruben Amorim
arts.sum: CHRIS WHEELER: United have announced another round of redundancies that could take the total number of job losses to 450 since Sir Jim Ratcliffe and Ineos began running football operations.

arts.ttl: Rasmus Hojlund’s goal drought due to Manchester United’s ‘lack of threat’
arts.sum: Hojlund has only scored two goals in the Premier League this season which Ruben Amorim attributed to the lack of chances created for him

arts.ttl: Erik ten Hag hints he may NEVER return to management as ex-Man Utd boss makes career change
arts.sum: Manchester United finally sacked Erik ten Hag in October after he was linked with an exit for months and the Dutchman is yet to take his next job as he weighs up his next move

arts.ttl: 'Look in the mirror' - Erik ten Hag makes new job admission following Man United sack
arts.sum: Former Manchester United head coach Erik ten Hag has spoken about his next job - four months since leaving Old Trafford

arts.ttl: 'Hard choices are necessary' - Man Utd to cut up to 200 more jobs
arts.sum: After 250 members of staff were made redundant last year, Manchester United announce up to 200 more redundancies to "return the club to profitability".
